APIKeyRotateResponse.description:
Rotate response = create-key shape + ``old_key_expires_at``.

Inherits from ``APIKeyResponse`` so the rotate route preserves the
full pre-existing field set (``last_used_at``, ``usage_count``,
``expires_at``, ``revoked_at``) on top of adding the grace-period
expiry. That matters for callers that diff a rotate response against
their cached ``APIKey`` model — dropping fields would silently
null-out parts of their state.
APIKeyRotateResponse.title: APIKeyRotateResponse

BalanceResponse.description: Current credit balance, plan, and billing-period summary. ``estimated_minutes_remaining`` is derived from the balance (1 credit ≈ 1 agent minute).

credit_balance.description: Authoritative credit balance, as a decimal string to preserve precision for JavaScript clients.
credit_balance.title: Credit Balance

dunning.description: Stripe dunning timeline when ``payment_status='past_due'``. ``None`` for healthy / canceled tenants and for tenants we couldn't reach Stripe for at request time.

estimated_minutes_remaining.description: Approximate agent-call minutes the current balance covers, computed at the standard 1 credit per minute conversion.
estimated_minutes_remaining.title: Estimated Minutes Remaining

payg_state.description: Pay-as-you-go state when ``credit_balance < 0``. ``None`` when balance is non-negative (no overage).

payment_status.description: Payment status: ``active`` (healthy), ``past_due`` (card declined; Stripe is retrying), or ``canceled`` (retries exhausted or chargeback).
payment_status.title: Payment Status

subscription_interval.description: Billing cadence of the tenant's active Stripe subscription: ``monthly``, ``yearly``, or ``null`` (free tier or no paid subscription).
subscription_interval.title: Subscription Interval

BalanceResponse.title: BalanceResponse

DeveloperSessionSummary.description:
A single active session for the authenticated developer.

``id`` is a short, non-secret identifier for the session and cannot be used as a credential. Forensic fields (``created_ip``, ``last_activity_ip``, ``user_agent``) are best-effort and may be null.
DeveloperSessionSummary.title: DeveloperSessionSummary

DunningState.description: Stripe dunning timeline for a ``past_due`` tenant, fetched live from Stripe. ``null`` for tenants that are not ``past_due``.

attempt_count.description: How many retries Stripe has burned on this invoice.
attempt_count.title: Attempt Count

final_cutoff_at.description: When the subscription will be auto-canceled if dunning still fails. Mirrors ``subscription.cancel_at`` from Stripe; ``None`` if Stripe hasn't scheduled an auto-cancel yet.
final_cutoff_at.title: Final Cutoff At

last_failed_at.description: When Stripe last attempted the dunning charge and got declined.
last_failed_at.title: Last Failed At

next_retry_at.description: When Stripe will attempt the next dunning retry. ``None`` when Stripe has exhausted retries (final cutoff reached).
next_retry_at.title: Next Retry At

DunningState.title: DunningState

InvoiceEntry.description:
A single row in the billing history. Subscription rows are Stripe invoices; pay-as-you-go overage rows are Stripe receipts.

``type`` distinguishes subscription invoices from overage receipts. ``status`` is the Stripe invoice status for subscription rows and ``paid`` for overage receipts. ``amount_paid`` and ``amount_due`` are in USD cents. ``created`` / ``period_start`` / ``period_end`` are Unix epoch seconds. ``hosted_invoice_url`` links to the Stripe-hosted document; ``invoice_pdf`` is a direct PDF link when Stripe provides one (null for overage rows and draft invoices).
InvoiceEntry.title: InvoiceEntry

InvoiceListResponse.description: ``GET /billing/invoices`` payload. Cursor-paginated: pass ``next_cursor`` back as ``?cursor=...`` (``?next_cursor=...`` is also accepted). When ``next_cursor`` is null, the oldest billing document has been reached.
InvoiceListResponse.title: InvoiceListResponse

MagicLinkRequestResponse.description:
Identical body for all well-formed requests.

Email sign-in creates an account only after the ownership-proof link
is verified. The request step returns the same acknowledgement for
new and returning developers.

message.default: A sign-in link is on its way.
message.title: Message

MagicLinkRequestResponse.title: MagicLinkRequestResponse

ModelEntry.description:
One row of the public LLM catalog.

``available`` reflects whether the deployment has the upstream API key
needed to route this model. The default catalog filters unavailable
entries so SDK pickers don't advertise something that will silently fall
through to local vLLM at call time. Pass ``?include_unavailable=true``
to see every public name the platform knows about, regardless of
deployment configuration.
ModelEntry.title: ModelEntry

PaygState.description:
Pay-as-you-go (overage) accounting for the current period.

Populated when ``credit_balance`` is negative. Tenants without a payment method see the same negative balance but cannot be charged until a card is added.

last_charge_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful PAYG charge.
last_charge_at.title: Last Charge At

pending_overage_credits.description: Absolute value of the negative balance, in credits. Always >= 0; the sign is implied (overage).
pending_overage_credits.title: Pending Overage Credits

pending_overage_usd.description: Pending overage in USD at the current PAYG rate (``PAYG_OVERAGE_RATE_USD_PER_MINUTE`` = $0.065/credit today).
pending_overage_usd.title: Pending Overage Usd

threshold_credits.description: Credit threshold above which the next pay-as-you-go charge fires.
threshold_credits.title: Threshold Credits

PaygState.title: PaygState

has_payment_method.description: True when the tenant has at least one card or Stripe Link payment method attached.
has_payment_method.title: Has Payment Method

PaymentMethodSummary.description:
A trimmed view of a saved payment method.

Covers both card and Stripe Link payment methods. No full card number, PAN, or CVV is ever included. For cards, the brand, last4, and expiry fields are populated; for Link payment methods those are null and ``link_email`` carries the Link account email.

is_default.description: True for the tenant's default payment method — the one Stripe charges for renewals, proration, and retries.
is_default.title: Is Default

link_email.description: Email associated with the Stripe Link PaymentMethod. Null for non-Link PMs.
link_email.title: Link Email

type.description: Stripe PaymentMethod ``type``. ``card`` and ``link`` are the supported values; other Stripe types are not accepted because they do not support off-session recurring charges.
type.title: Type

wallet_type.description: Wallet identifier for cards created via a digital wallet (``apple_pay``, ``google_pay``, ``samsung_pay``, ``link``). Null for non-wallet cards and non-card payment methods.
wallet_type.title: Wallet Type

PaymentMethodSummary.title: PaymentMethodSummary

PeriodSummary.description:
Snapshot of the current billing period: start and end, plus the grant/used/remaining decomposition.

``credits_granted`` is the plan's monthly allocation, not a sum of grants landed this period (a mid-period upgrade reflects the plan tier). ``credits_used`` is the sum of usage deductions since the period start; signup, renewal, and manual adjustments are excluded. ``credits_remaining`` mirrors the credit balance and can exceed ``credits_granted`` after a pay-as-you-go top-up.
PeriodSummary.title: PeriodSummary

PlanCatalogEntry.description:
A single tier in the public plan catalogue.

``yearly_price_usd`` is null on tiers without a yearly variant (such as the free trial). ``is_trial`` plans grant credits once at signup and never refill; currently only the free plan is a trial.
PlanCatalogEntry.title: PlanCatalogEntry

PlanInfo.description: Compact plan view embedded in the balance response. The full catalogue is available at ``GET /billing/plans``.
PlanInfo.title: PlanInfo

PlanListResponse.description: ``GET /billing/plans`` payload. ``current_plan_slug`` identifies the tenant's current tier so a client can highlight it without a second ``/billing/balance`` call.
PlanListResponse.title: PlanListResponse

PresignUploadRequest.description: Ask the server for a presigned URL the client can PUT a file to.
PresignUploadRequest.title: PresignUploadRequest

SelectPlanRequest.description:
Optional body for ``POST /billing/plans/{slug}/select``.

Defaults to monthly billing. Yearly is rejected with 400 if the
requested plan has no yearly variant configured (currently the
free trial). The interval drives which ``stripe_price_id`` the
backend hands to ``create_subscription`` /
``update_subscription_price`` and which ``Stripe Price`` Stripe
bills against.
SelectPlanRequest.title: SelectPlanRequest

SelectPlanResponse.description:
``POST /billing/plans/{slug}/select`` payload.

Returns the new state after the Stripe subscription is created, updated, or scheduled for cancellation. ``status`` is one of:

  * A Stripe subscription status (``active``, ``trialing``, ``incomplete``, ``past_due``, …).
  * ``free`` — an already-free tenant re-affirmed the free tier; no Stripe call was needed.
  * ``scheduled_cancellation`` — a downgrade from a paid tier to free; the subscription stays active through the paid period.

When ``status`` is ``incomplete`` (first charge declined), the response includes ``payment_intent_client_secret`` so the client can confirm the payment. The plan only advances to the new tier after Stripe transitions the subscription to ``active``.

awaiting_payment.description: True when a paid-to-paid upgrade is pending payment of the proration invoice. ``status`` still reads ``active`` (the subscription is active on the old price); confirm the PaymentIntent to apply the upgrade.
awaiting_payment.title: Awaiting Payment

payment_intent_client_secret.description: Stripe PaymentIntent client secret for the first invoice. Present when ``status`` is ``incomplete``; pass it to the client to confirm the existing payment (do not create a new subscription on retry, which would double-charge).
payment_intent_client_secret.title: Payment Intent Client Secret

SelectPlanResponse.title: SelectPlanResponse

SetupIntentResponse.description: Returned so the client's Stripe Payment Element can confirm card details. ``client_secret`` is the value the Payment Element needs; treat it as sensitive.

client_secret.description: Stripe SetupIntent client secret. Pass to the Stripe Payment Element as the ``clientSecret`` option.
client_secret.title: Client Secret

publishable_key.description: Stripe publishable key (``pk_*``), returned alongside the client secret so the client does not need a separate config call.
publishable_key.title: Publishable Key

SetupIntentResponse.title: SetupIntentResponse

TransactionEntry.description: A single ledger row. ``details`` is the row's metadata passed through opaquely; clients can render whatever fields it contains (for example ``call_id``, ``tts_chars``, ``asr_seconds``, ``payment_intent_id``) without relying on a fixed shape.

amount.description: Signed credit movement: negative for usage / charges, positive for grants / top-ups.
amount.title: Amount

balance_after.description: Tenant balance after this entry was applied.
balance_after.title: Balance After

details.description: Free-form metadata (call_id, resource counts, idempotency key, etc). Shape is not guaranteed and may change per tx_type.
details.title: Details

TransactionEntry.title: TransactionEntry

TransactionListResponse.description: ``GET /billing/transactions`` payload. Cursor-paginated: pass ``next_cursor`` back as ``?cursor=...`` (``?next_cursor=...`` is also accepted). When ``next_cursor`` is null, the oldest entry has been reached.
TransactionListResponse.title: TransactionListResponse

TwilioAccountCreate.description:
Request body for ``POST /api/v1/twilio-accounts``.

``auth_token`` is required at registration time so we can verify the
SID/token pair with Twilio before persisting.
TwilioAccountCreate.title: TwilioAccountCreate

TwilioAccountResponse.description:
Response shape. ``auth_token`` is never returned.

``tenant_id`` is ``None`` for platform-shared credentials (any tenant
can attach numbers) and a UUID string for tenant-owned (BYO) rows.
TwilioAccountResponse.title: TwilioAccountResponse

UsageByResourceEntry.description:
One bucket value in ``UsageSummary.by_resource_type``.

Keys mirror what the usage tracker aggregates per ``resource_type``:
request count, token totals, audio seconds, and TTS input
characters. All fields default to zero so an empty bucket is a
well-typed object rather than a partial dict.
UsageByResourceEntry.title: UsageByResourceEntry

total_audio_seconds.description: Sum of audio_seconds across ALL resource types: voice calls, transcription input audio, and any other rows that record an audio duration. For agent-active time alone, use total_voice_seconds.
total_audio_seconds.title: Total Audio Seconds

total_request_duration_ms.description: Sum of HTTP request duration_ms across every non-voice REST endpoint under ``/api/v1/*``. Voice calls are not HTTP requests; see total_voice_seconds.
total_request_duration_ms.title: Total Request Duration Ms

total_voice_seconds.description: Sum of audio_seconds across voice_call rows. This is the agent-active time captured by the voice pipeline at end-of-call.
total_voice_seconds.title: Total Voice Seconds

UsageTriggerFiringResponse.description:
One row from ``GET /usage/triggers/{id}/firings``.

``payload`` is the exact JSON we POSTed to the customer's URL so they
can replay it against their handler when debugging a missed event.
``delivery_status`` reflects the final state after retries:
``delivered`` / ``failed`` / ``pending``.
UsageTriggerFiringResponse.title: UsageTriggerFiringResponse

UsageTriggerUpdate.description:
Partial update for ``PATCH /usage/triggers/{id}``.

Only fields a customer can re-tune in place are exposed: the
``is_active`` flag (deactivate to recover the per-tenant cap
without losing audit history), the threshold value (re-tune the
trip point as their volume scales), and the callback URL (rotate
the receiver). The trigger's *shape* — ``resource_type``,
``threshold_type``, ``period`` — is immutable; a different shape
is logically a different trigger and should be created fresh so
the firings audit table cleanly tracks one configuration over
time.

Every field is Optional; PATCH is partial. An empty body is a
valid no-op.

``extra='forbid'`` rejects unknown keys with HTTP 422. This catches
two real customer foot-guns at once: PATCHing an immutable shape
field (``resource_type`` / ``threshold_type`` / ``period``) used
to return 200 with the value silently dropped, and a misspelled
editable field name (e.g. ``threshold_val``) used to return 200
with no clue the change was ignored. Both now surface as an
explicit validation error at the boundary.
UsageTriggerUpdate.title: UsageTriggerUpdate

text.default: Hello, this is a sample of my voice.
text.title: Text

info.description: Unified gateway for voice AI operations
info.title: Aethex Voice API

post.description: Deep-copy an agent including tools, knowledge docs, and chunks.
Ingest a knowledge-base document via a presigned upload (no WAF body inspection).

get.description:
Get extracted text from all processed knowledge base documents.

Returns the format expected by PipelineConfig.knowledge_documents for RAG.

post.description: Re-extract and re-embed a knowledge base document.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Api V1 Api Keys Get

parameters.description: When false (default), the old key keeps working for a 24-hour grace period so callers can roll out the new secret without downtime; the grace expiry is returned as ``old_key_expires_at``. Set to true to hard-revoke the old key in the same call (suspected compromise); ``old_key_expires_at`` will be null in that case.
parameters.name: revoke_immediately

schema.description: When false (default), the old key keeps working for a 24-hour grace period so callers can roll out the new secret without downtime; the grace expiry is returned as ``old_key_expires_at``. Set to true to hard-revoke the old key in the same call (suspected compromise); ``old_key_expires_at`` will be null in that case.
schema.title: Revoke Immediately

post.description: Authenticate via Google SSO. Creates account on first sign-in.

Log out and revoke the current session.

Rate-limited per session.

Revoke every active session for the developer.

Rate-limited per-developer so a stolen access token cannot be used
to repeatedly burn the legitimate user's recovery path. The cookies
on this response are cleared because the caller's own session is
among those revoked.

Request a one-time sign-in link by email.

Always returns the same body on well-formed input. If the address has no
developer row yet, the service stores only a pending email token; the
account is created after the token is verified. 429 remains generic so
limiter state does not become an address-discovery surface.

Consume a magic-link token and return a developer JWT pair (and, on first sign-in, an initial API key).

The response shape matches the Google sign-in response so clients can handle both sign-in methods identically. Rate-limited per IP.

delete.description: Soft-delete developer account and deactivate all API keys.

get.description:
Get current developer profile.

Requires JWT bearer token (not API key). Use Google or email magic-link sign-in
to obtain a token.

post.description:
Rotate the developer's access/refresh token pair.

Accepts the refresh token from the ``aethex_refresh_token`` HttpOnly cookie (preferred) or from the request body (for non-browser callers). The refresh token stays valid after the access token has expired, which is what lets this endpoint mint a new access token.

Requests are rate-limited. Cookie-based calls are additionally CSRF-protected with a double-submit token (the ``X-CSRF-Token`` header must match the ``aethex_csrf_token`` cookie); body-token callers are exempt.

get.description:
List the authenticated developer's active sessions.

Returns one row per session for a "where you're signed in" view. ``is_current`` marks the session backing the current request.
Current credit balance, plan, and billing-period summary.

List the tenant's billing documents, newest first.

Subscription charges come from Stripe invoices; pay-as-you-go overage charges link to the corresponding Stripe receipt. Documents are generated and hosted by Stripe.

Returns an empty list when the tenant has no billing documents yet (for example, a free trial that never selected a paid plan). Returns 503 when billing is not configured for the environment.

parameters.description: Stripe invoice id to page after (received as ``next_cursor`` on the previous page). Omit for the first page. Also accepted as ``?next_cursor=`` for clients that echo the response field name directly.
parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Stripe invoice id to page after (received as ``next_cursor`` on the previous page). Omit for the first page. Also accepted as ``?next_cursor=`` for clients that echo the response field name directly.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Alias for ``cursor``. Accepted so a client reading the response field ``next_cursor`` and passing it back works without surprises. Prefer ``cursor``.
parameters.name: next_cursor

schema.description: Alias for ``cursor``. Accepted so a client reading the response field ``next_cursor`` and passing it back works without surprises. Prefer ``cursor``.
schema.title: Next Cursor

post.description:
Return a Stripe SetupIntent the client can confirm to attach a card.

The SetupIntent is created for off-session use so the saved card can later be charged for pay-as-you-go overage without re-prompting. The Stripe customer is created on first use.

get.description:
Cards on file for the tenant. Empty list when no Stripe
Customer has been created yet.

delete.description:
Detach a payment method from the tenant.

Ownership is verified before the payment method is detached; a payment method that does not belong to the authenticated tenant returns 404 (indistinguishable from a non-existent id). On a Stripe error, the response surfaces a user-facing message when one is available.

get.description: Return the plan catalogue and the tenant's current tier.

post.description:
Switch the tenant's subscription to the named plan tier.

First-time selection of a paid tier creates a new Stripe
Subscription against the tenant's saved payment method. Subsequent
selections update the existing Subscription's price item with
pro-ration. Selecting the free tier cancels the active
Subscription (the renewal job continues granting free-tier
credits).

Required state:
  * Tenant must have ``has_payment_method=True`` (i.e. attached a
    chargeable card or Link PM via the SetupIntent flow) before
    selecting any public self-serve tier, including the free tier.
    Paid-to-free cancellation is still allowed because it schedules
    the existing subscription to end.
  * Plan row must have ``stripe_price_id`` populated (ops sets
    this after creating the Stripe Price). If not populated for
    the requested tier, the route returns 503.

get.description: Paginated credit ledger, newest-first.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from the previous page's ``next_cursor``. Also accepted as ``?next_cursor=`` for clients that echo the response field name directly.
parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque cursor from the previous page's ``next_cursor``. Also accepted as ``?next_cursor=`` for clients that echo the response field name directly.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Alias for ``cursor``. Accepted so a client reading the response field ``next_cursor`` and passing it back as a query param works without surprises. Prefer ``cursor``.
parameters.name: next_cursor

schema.description: Alias for ``cursor``. Accepted so a client reading the response field ``next_cursor`` and passing it back as a query param works without surprises. Prefer ``cursor``.
schema.title: Next Cursor

post.description: Create a call record (does not place the call).

Dispatch a batch of outbound calls (provider-agnostic).

Optional scheduling: ``start_at`` defers the dispatch until the given
timestamp; ``end_at`` causes the worker to abandon any unstarted
recipients past that deadline. Both are timezone-aware ISO 8601.
Returns a ``batch_id`` for polling via ``GET /calls/batch/{id}``.

get.description: Return the current status of a batch dispatch.

post.description:
Create a call record and schedule the outbound dial (async / 202).

Returns **202 Accepted** with the Call record (``status="queued"``)
as soon as the row is persisted. The actual provider dial — which
includes an up-to-300 s wait for TTS to become ready — runs as a
background task so the HTTP response is never held open past the
typical SDK timeout (30–60 s). Clients poll ``GET /calls/{id}`` for
terminal status. On TTS-readiness failure the row is marked
``failed`` and ``metadata.error_message`` carries the user-facing
explanation.

get.description:
Return telephony status for a call.

``status`` is one of ``queued``, ``ringing``, ``in-progress``, ``completed``, ``failed``, ``no-answer``, ``busy``, or ``canceled``. It is typed as a string rather than a closed enum so historical values round-trip cleanly; the ``GET /calls?status=...`` filter is strict and rejects unknown values with 422.

post.description:
Create an authenticated WebRTC session.

Agent configuration is resolved on the server; the client receives only a session id and ICE configuration, never the agent configuration.

Accepts either an ``X-API-Key`` or a developer ``Authorization: Bearer`` JWT. With a developer JWT, a session may only be created against agents the developer owns.

Gracefully end an active WebRTC session.

Signals the session to shut down cleanly. Accepts either an ``X-API-Key`` or a developer ``Authorization: Bearer`` JWT (see ``connect``).

patch.description:
Submit a trickled ICE candidate for an active session.

This signaling endpoint is quota-exempt — it is not charged against your API rate limit, since ICE trickle is time-sensitive and can be high volume — but callers must present a valid ``calls:write`` API key.

post.description:
Submit SDP offer for an authenticated session.

Verifies session ownership, then starts the pipeline with the config
that was locked in at /connect time. Client config overrides are
ignored. Accepts either ``X-API-Key`` or a developer JWT — see
``connect`` for the rationale.

get.description:
Get the status of an active or completed session.

Returns status, duration, and turn count for both active and completed sessions. Session data is retained for a limited time after the session ends.

post.description:
Submit a tool call result back to an active session.

When the agent's model calls a client-side tool, the client runs it and returns the result here. The result is added to the model context as a tool response and the conversation continues.

get.description: Text search across conversation turns using ILIKE.

Return a short-lived signed URL for the conversation audio.

The URL is served by the API and carries a signed ``token`` query parameter, so it can be played directly in a browser ``<audio>`` element without auth headers. The token has a limited lifetime. The URL extension and the ``format`` field reflect the recording's format (typically ``wav``) so clients can select a decoder.

Stream the conversation audio.

The ``Content-Type`` reflects the underlying recording format (typically ``wav``).

Authenticate with either a signed ``?token=`` issued by ``GET /audio`` (which lets a browser embed the URL in an ``<audio>`` element without headers) or a normal ``X-API-Key`` / ``Authorization: Bearer`` key with the ``conversations:read`` scope.

post.description:
Revoke a previously issued audio token before it expires.

Use this to invalidate an audio link that was shared accidentally, without waiting for it to expire. Returns 204 on success and is idempotent (re-revoking is a no-op). Returns 404 when the token is not valid for this conversation, so callers cannot probe for valid tokens.
Store feedback (rating + optional comment) in conversation metadata.

schema.title: Response Get Transcript Api V1 Conversations  Conversation Id  Transcript Get

get.description:
Return the public LLM model catalog.

``aethex-default`` selects the platform default model. When ``?include_unavailable=true`` is set, models whose provider is not currently available are included with ``available: false``.

parameters.description: Include models whose upstream provider key is not configured on this deployment. Default false so SDK model pickers only see names that will actually route externally.
parameters.name: include_unavailable

schema.description: Include models whose upstream provider key is not configured on this deployment. Default false so SDK model pickers only see names that will actually route externally.
schema.title: Include Unavailable
Response List Models Api V1 Models Get

post.description:
Register a Twilio phone number under a tenant-owned Twilio account.

Supply a ``twilio_account_id`` that resolves to an active Twilio account owned by this tenant. Those credentials are used to bind the number's Voice URL on Twilio and are stored so later update/release calls can reuse them.

Status codes:
  * 404 — ``agent_id`` (when supplied) does not exist for this tenant.
  * 422 — ``twilio_account_id`` is invalid, cross-tenant, or released, or the phone number is not in the supplied Twilio account.
  * 503 — the Twilio credentials could not be used (for example, Twilio was unreachable or timed out).
Async transcription consuming a presigned upload.

Transcribe a file previously staged via /uploads/presign.

Bodies don't transit the ALB/WAF — only a small JSON reference does.

delete.description: Cancel a queued transcription job. Returns 409 if already running/completed.

post.description:
Batch synthesis — submit multiple texts, get a batch_id for polling.

Audio files are stored in S3. Poll GET /tts/batch/{batch_id} for
presigned download URLs when complete.

Usage metering is deferred to the worker (run_tts_batch) so only
successfully synthesized items are billed.

get.description: Poll batch synthesis status. Returns presigned audio URLs for completed items.

post.description:
Streaming synthesis — returns chunked PCM16 audio at 24kHz.

Lower TTFB than POST /tts. Audio starts arriving as it's generated.
Supports voice_id for voice cloning.

Register a Twilio Account SID for this tenant.

Validates the supplied ``account_sid`` and ``auth_token`` by fetching the account from Twilio, then stores the SID and an encrypted copy of the auth token (used at runtime for inbound webhook signature verification and outbound calls).

Status codes:
  * 422 — Twilio rejected the credentials, or the supplied SID does not exist or does not match.
  * 409 — the Account SID is already registered, or the tenant has reached its account limit.
  * 429 — registration rate limit exceeded.
  * 503 — the registration could not be validated (for example, Twilio was unreachable or timed out).

delete.description:
Release a registered Twilio account, freeing its Account SID slot.

Idempotent: returns 204 whether the account was active, already released, or never existed under this tenant.

post.description: Binary bodies bypass the API ALB/WAF. After the PUT succeeds, pass `upload_id` to the consuming endpoint (e.g. `POST /transcribe/by-upload`).
post.summary: Get a presigned URL to upload a file directly to storage

parameters.description: Restrict the aggregate to usage rows logged against this API key. Always tenant-scoped: a key id that belongs to a different tenant (or doesn't exist) returns zeros rather than leaking existence.
parameters.name: api_key_id

schema.description: Restrict the aggregate to usage rows logged against this API key. Always tenant-scoped: a key id that belongs to a different tenant (or doesn't exist) returns zeros rather than leaking existence.
schema.title: Api Key Id

get.description:
Per-day usage rollup for the trailing N calendar days.

``days`` is a **date window**, not a row limit: the response always
contains exactly ``days`` entries, one per calendar day in UTC,
newest-first. Days with no activity are returned as zero-filled
rows so the response is chart-ready without client-side gap
handling.

The 1..365 bound is enforced by Pydantic; values outside that
range 422 at the boundary.

parameters.description: Restrict the aggregate to usage rows logged against this API key. Always tenant-scoped: a key id that belongs to a different tenant (or doesn't exist) returns zeros rather than leaking existence.
parameters.name: api_key_id

schema.description: Restrict the aggregate to usage rows logged against this API key. Always tenant-scoped: a key id that belongs to a different tenant (or doesn't exist) returns zeros rather than leaking existence.
schema.title: Api Key Id
Response Get Daily Usage Api V1 Usage Daily Get

parameters.description: Restrict the aggregate to usage rows logged against this API key. Always tenant-scoped: a key id that belongs to a different tenant (or doesn't exist) returns zeros rather than leaking existence.

get.description: Alias for GET /usage.

parameters.description: Restrict the aggregate to usage rows logged against this API key. Always tenant-scoped: a key id that belongs to a different tenant (or doesn't exist) returns zeros rather than leaking existence.

schema.description: Restrict the aggregate to usage rows logged against this API key. Always tenant-scoped: a key id that belongs to a different tenant (or doesn't exist) returns zeros rather than leaking existence.

Response List Triggers Api V1 Usage Triggers Get

post.description:
Configure a usage threshold and callback URL.

When the threshold is crossed for the period, a signed webhook is posted to the callback URL. A trigger fires once per period and re-arms when the period rolls over.

Returns 409 when the tenant has reached the maximum number of active triggers (deactivate one to free a slot). Returns 400 when the tenant has no webhook secret configured — set one with ``POST /usage/webhook-secret/rotate``.

patch.description:
Update a trigger's mutable fields.

Editable fields: ``is_active``, ``threshold_value``, and ``event_callback_url``. The trigger shape (``resource_type``, ``threshold_type``, ``period``) is immutable; create a new trigger to change it.

Returns 404 when the trigger does not exist or belongs to another tenant. Returns 400 when ``event_callback_url`` is invalid or resolves to a non-public address. Reactivating a trigger re-checks the per-tenant active cap and that its ``resource_type`` is still eligible (otherwise 422). An empty body is a valid no-op.

get.description:
Audit log for a single trigger's firing attempts.

Newest-first. ``limit`` is enforced 1..200 by Pydantic; values
outside that range 422 at the boundary. Returns 404 if the trigger
isn't owned by the calling tenant so a customer can't probe other
tenants' trigger ids by guessing.

schema.title: Response List Trigger Firings Api V1 Usage Triggers  Trigger Id  Firings Get

post.description:
Re-attempt webhook delivery for a single firing.

Replays the EXACT bytes we POSTed the first time so the receiver
sees identical signature + payload. Updates the same audit row
in place (incrementing ``attempt_count``, refreshing
``delivery_status`` / ``http_status`` / ``last_error``); we do
NOT insert a new firing row because each row represents one
"threshold crossed for this period" event and a redeliver is the
same logical event, just retried.

404 when trigger or firing isn't owned by the calling tenant
(no cross-tenant probing).
400 when the tenant has rotated their webhook_secret and the old
signature would no longer verify on the receiver — refuse the
redeliver because the receiver would silently reject it as
tampered. Customer recovery: configure a new trigger or wait for
the next natural fire (which will use the new secret).

Generate (or replace) the tenant-level webhook signing secret.

Tenant-owned webhook bodies are signed with this secret via
HMAC-SHA256 (``X-Aethex-Signature`` header). This includes usage
triggers, async transcription callbacks, and TTS batch callbacks.
The secret is returned exactly once — store it securely. Rotating
replaces it immediately; in-flight deliveries signed with the old
secret will fail HMAC verification on the receiver side until you
update your handler.

A tenant that has never called this endpoint has no signing secret;
the trigger evaluator records those firings as ``failed`` with
``last_error='tenant has no webhook_secret configured'``.

Concurrency: the tenant row is locked with ``SELECT ... FOR UPDATE``
for the duration of this transaction so two simultaneous rotate
calls serialise. Without the lock both callers could read the same
pre-state, generate different new secrets, and only the last
commit's secret would persist — the earlier caller would walk
away with a secret that is already invalid and every webhook
delivery to them would HMAC-mismatch.

schema.title: Response List Voices Api V1 Voices Get

get.description:
Return the set of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes accepted by the ``?country=`` filter on ``GET /voices``.

Each entry is a ``{"code": "NG", "name": "Nigeria"}`` object, sorted by code, so clients can render a country picker without hardcoding the list. Requires the ``voices:read`` scope.

schema.title: Response List Countries Api V1 Voices Countries Get

get.description:
Return the closed vocabulary of voice tags.

Requires the ``voices:read`` scope. Tags are grouped into categories (``tone``, ``voice_texture``, ``delivery_style``, ``business_persona``) for display; ``GET /voices?tag=...`` accepts any tag from any category. Tags outside this set are rejected with a 422 that points back to this endpoint, so this is the canonical place to discover supported tags.

schema.title: Response List Tag Vocabulary Api V1 Voices Tag Vocabulary Get
